chapter_context.summary: Paul recounts his ministry in Thessalonica, emphasizing his transparent, parental care and the fact that he worked to support himself so as not to be a burden.

outline.explanation: This section focuses on Paul's Conduct and Ministry (2:1-12), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 Thessalonians.

outline.text: The Thessalonians' Reception of the Word
outline.explanation: This section focuses on The Thessalonians' Reception of the Word (2:13-16), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 Thessalonians.

outline.text: Paul's Great Longing to See Them
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Paul's Great Longing to See Them (2:17-20), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 Thessalonians.

1.commentary:
### Tyndale Study Notes
**Chapter Overview: 1 Thessalonians 2**
This verse contributes to the central message of 1 Thessalonians, emphasizing the grace and truth revealed in Jesus Christ.
1.full_commentary:
### Matthew Henry Commentary
Matthew Henry offers devotional reflections on the spiritual impact of this passage for the believer.

### Jamieson-Fausset-Brown
JFB analysis explores the historical context and original language nuances of this portion of the New Testament.

devotional.question: How does this passage impact your daily walk with God?

cultural_historical.title: New Testament Context
cultural_historical.text: Written in the 1st century AD to diverse groups of believers across the Roman Empire.
